title: specklinia ichthyonekys
side_effects: Specklinia ichthyonekys can have side effects if used improperly, such as excessive bleeding or bruising when applied topically due to its high concentration of anti-coagulant compounds. Ingestion of the plant can cause stomach upset, nausea, and vomiting. Prolonged exposure to the plant's oils may also lead to skin irritation, allergic reactions, and increased sensitivity to sunlight. Furthermore, improper use of Specklinia ichthyonekys may interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners, and exacerbate underlying health conditions.
